headlines.title: Chris Mason: Another Starmer critic gets promotion as Sarwar joins Burnham government
headlines.description: Months after calling on Keir Starmer to quit, the Scottish Labour leader is set to become a minister in Burnham's government, the BBC's political editor writes.

headlines.title: PC Harper killers' potential early release is an 'insult', says his mother
headlines.description: PC Harper, 28, sustained fatal injuries while responding to a quad bike theft in Berkshire in 2019.

headlines.title: Ukrainian drones hit more sites of Russian online retailer Wildberries
headlines.description: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight.

headlines.title: Food prices have fallen – but inflation expected to rise from here
headlines.description: June's fall in inflation will be welcomed by the new prime minister but analysts warn the fall is temporary.

headlines.title: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
headlines.description: Wilson was accused of defaming young Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, but cleared by a Sydney court.

headlines.title: Prince George poses in suit for new photo as he becomes a teenager
headlines.description: It's a big year for the young prince, who will start secondary school at the elite Eton College in September.

headlines.title: US renews strikes on Iran as Trump threatens to attack underground nuclear site
headlines.description: The US president's threat to target a key Iranian nuclear site came ahead of the 11th consecutive night of attacks.

headlines.title: Military kit still failing women, troops say
headlines.description: Servicewomen still face shortages of properly fitting kit, five years after a landmark report.

headlines.title: Nottingham attacks NHS trust faces manslaughter probe
headlines.description: The hospital trust treated Valdo Calocane before he killed three people in the city in 2023.

headlines.title: Streeting apologises after early prisoner release comments heard on mic
headlines.description: The defence secretary and Mahmood were also heard making jokes about the new justice secretary.

headlines.title: Great Apes kiss and cuddle to keep the peace, says study
headlines.description: Hugging before stressful events may be an ancient way of building trust, say scientists studying apes.

headlines.title: I've got a motorway in my garden - it's my shelter in hot weather
headlines.description: The road was built over people's homes 60 years ago, but what's it like to live in the shadow of the M4?

headlines.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but some women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
headlines.description: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.

headlines.title: Will your job be replaced by AI? Here are the roles most affected
headlines.description: Artificial Intelligence companies make vast claims about their tools replacing human labour - our charts show what's happening.

headlines.title: 'Play music loud to remember Ozzy' says Sharon on first anniversary of his death
headlines.description: Sharon Osbourne shares with the BBC her memories and hopes for the future.

headlines.title: From singing in a bar to performing as an alien in new Supergirl film
headlines.description: Winnie Ama couldn't believe her luck when she had the chance to sing her original song in the new Supergirl movie - but there was a catch.

headlines.title: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

headlines.title: Who's in Andy Burnham's cabinet?
headlines.description: An in-depth look at which Labour MPs have been appointed to cabinet roles by new Prime Minister Andy Burnham

headlines.title: Watch: Beluga whales flown from closed Canadian marine park to new US home
headlines.description: Six beluga whales have arrived in the US after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

headlines.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
headlines.description: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

headlines.title: More women in their 20s are getting type 2 diabetes, experts warn
headlines.description: England is seeing "a worrying increase" in women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes, say experts. Here's what to look out for.

headlines.title: Godzilla vs Kong actress Kaylee Hottle dies in crash at 18
headlines.description: She portrayed a young deaf orphan named Jia in the 2021 blockbuster and its 2024 sequel.

headlines.title: 'We just need a bed': Baby with 'days to live' can't access lifesaving treatment
headlines.description: Dylan and Catrin Green fear baby Macs has days to live and say he urgently needs a Berlin heart.

headlines.title: Judge rules Trump must hand over records sought by BBC in defamation case
headlines.description: The judge grants a motion to compel the release of files held by the Donald J Trump Revocable Trust.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Andy Burnham's (un)funded tax cut
headlines.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham plans for a “cost of living government”.

headlines.title: Who is the new PM?
headlines.description: From Manchester mayor to prime minister; who is he and what does stand for?

headlines.title: Why Chelsea have signed so many Man City academy graduates
headlines.description: Morgan Rogers is the latest ex-Man City academy player to join Chelsea and will be reunited with more former City team-mates.

headlines.title: Five big names to look out for at Commonwealth Games
headlines.description: These are the stars to look out for across the 10 sports at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow.

headlines.title: Where are Newcastle in bid to be the best by 2030?
headlines.description: As the fifth anniversary of Newcastle's takeover approaches, BBC Sport assesses whether the project to be "the best" is still on track.

headlines.title: Chelsea sign £117m Rogers - what will he bring?
headlines.description: Chelsea's £117m move for Aston Villa midfielder Morgan Rogers is a genuine transfer window shock - so how did they do it and what does it mean for the rest of the squad?

headlines.title: Littler throws nine-darter in superb Matchplay win
headlines.description: World number one Luke Littler throws a thrilling nine-darter as he books his place in the quarter-finals of the World Matchplay with a 11-8 win over Nathan Aspinall.

headlines.title: Arsenal sign Spain goalkeeper Rodriguez on free
headlines.description: Arsenal bring in Spain goalkeeper Misa Rodriguez on a free transfer - the Women's Super League side's sixth signing of the summer.

headlines.title: Adversity to history - Agyarko's world title dream
headlines.description: Belfast's Caoimhin Agyarko's dream is to become Ireland's first black professional boxing champion and he can reach his goal when he challenges Josh Kelly for the IBF light-middleweight title on Saturday.
